questions.q: In which field of Indian cinema is Shyam Benegal (born 1934) best known?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal is celebrated as a film director and a leading figure of India's Parallel cinema movement.

questions.q: Which 1974 film marked Shyam Benegal's feature directorial debut?
questions.explanation: Ankur (1974) was Shyam Benegal's debut feature film and a landmark of Parallel cinema.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal is widely regarded as a pioneering architect of which movement in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal was a foundational figure of the realist Parallel cinema (New Indian Cinema) of the 1970s.

questions.q: In which year was Shyam Benegal born?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal was born on 14 December 1934 in Hyderabad.

questions.q: Which lifetime achievement honour, India's highest award in cinema, did Shyam Benegal receive for the year 2005?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for 2005, India's highest honour in cinema, for his contribution to Indian film.

questions.q: Which actress made her film debut in Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974) and became a recurring face in his films?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi made her acting debut in Ankur (1974), launching a long association with Shyam Benegal's cinema.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Shyam Benegal born?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal was born in Hyderabad.

questions.q: Which 1976 Shyam Benegal film centred on a milk cooperative and the dairy movement in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Manthan (1976) dramatised the dairy cooperative movement and was inspired by the work of Verghese Kurien.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's acclaimed 1970s films such as Ankur and Nishant are best described as belonging to which genre?
questions.explanation: Benegal's early films were social realist dramas examining caste, feudalism and rural power structures.

questions.q: Which actor, who frequently worked with Shyam Benegal, played important roles in Nishant and Manthan?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah was a key actor in Benegal's films, appearing in Nishant, Manthan and others.

questions.q: Which 1975 Shyam Benegal film about feudal oppression in a village starred Girish Karnad, Shabana Azmi and Naseeruddin Shah?
questions.explanation: Nishant (1975) depicted feudal exploitation and competed for the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 1977 film Bhumika, about the life of an actress, was loosely based on the memoirs of which Marathi actress?
questions.explanation: Bhumika (1977) was inspired by 'Sangtye Aika', the autobiography of Marathi stage and screen actress Hansa Wadkar.

questions.q: Which actress won the National Film Award for Best Actress for her role in Shyam Benegal's Bhumika (1977)?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil won the National Film Award for Best Actress for her performance as Usha in Bhumika.

questions.q: Before directing feature films, in which advertising-related field did Shyam Benegal build his early career?
questions.explanation: Benegal worked extensively in advertising films and sponsored documentaries before his 1974 feature debut.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal directed an acclaimed multi-episode television series on Indian history that aired in the late 1980s. What was its title?
questions.explanation: Bharat Ek Khoj (1988), based on Nehru's The Discovery of India, was directed by Shyam Benegal for Doordarshan.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal directed a 2005 biographical film about which Indian independence leader, titled after him?
questions.explanation: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose: The Forgotten Hero (2005) was Benegal's biopic on the freedom fighter.

questions.q: Which music composer scored many of Shyam Benegal's early films, including Ankur, Nishant and Bhumika?
questions.explanation: Vanraj Bhatia was Benegal's regular music composer through much of his early and middle career.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's films Mammo (1994), Sardari Begum (1996) and Zubeidaa (2001) form a thematic trilogy focusing on the lives of which group?
questions.explanation: Mammo, Sardari Begum and Zubeidaa form Benegal's trilogy centred on the experiences of Muslim women, scripted by Khalid Mohamed.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 2001 film Zubeidaa, starring Karisma Kapoor in the title role, featured songs composed by which musician?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman composed the music for Zubeidaa (2001), including songs such as 'So Gaye Hain'.

questions.q: Which actor began his film career as the male lead in Shyam Benegal's debut Ankur (1974) and also starred in Nishant?
questions.explanation: Anant Nag made his film debut as the male lead in Benegal's Ankur (1974) and went on to star in Nishant.

questions.q: The financing of Shyam Benegal's Manthan (1976) is famous because it was funded by which body?
questions.explanation: Manthan was financed by around 500,000 Gujarat dairy farmers, each contributing a small sum, making it India's first crowdfunded film.

questions.q: Which cinematographer was a frequent collaborator with Shyam Benegal, shooting Ankur, Nishant and Bhumika?
questions.explanation: Govind Nihalani, who later became a noted director, was Benegal's cinematographer on his classic early films.

questions.q: In addition to the Padma Shri, which civilian honour was conferred on Shyam Benegal by the Government of India in 1991?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal received the Padma Shri in 1976 and the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian honour, in 1991.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's very first directorial work was a 1962 documentary titled Gher Betha Ganga, made in which language?
questions.explanation: Benegal directed his first documentary, Gher Betha Ganga, in Gujarati in 1962, early in his advertising-era career.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 1992 film Suraj Ka Satvan Ghoda was based on a novel by which Hindi author?
questions.explanation: Suraj Ka Satvan Ghoda (1992) was adapted from Dharamvir Bharati's novel of the same name.

questions.q: Which Shyam Benegal film set during the Revolt of 1857, produced by and starring Shashi Kapoor, won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi?
questions.explanation: Junoon (1979), produced by Shashi Kapoor and based on Ruskin Bond's A Flight of Pigeons, was set against the 1857 uprising and won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 1983 film Mandi, a satire set in a brothel, was led by which actress in the role of the madam Rukmini Bai?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi played the brothel madam Rukmini Bai in Benegal's ensemble satire Mandi (1983).

questions.q: From 2006 to 2012, Shyam Benegal served as a nominated Member of which house of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal was a nominated Member of the Rajya Sabha from 2006 to 2012.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 1985 film Trikal, featuring Naseeruddin Shah, was set against the backdrop of which Indian region's transition from colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Trikal (1985) was set in Goa around the end of Portuguese colonial rule, with a notable ensemble cast.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal directed a biographical drama, co-produced with Bangladesh, about which leader?
questions.explanation: Benegal directed Mujib: The Making of a Nation, a biopic of Bangladesh's founding leader Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, released in 2023.
